claim: For all integers n > 1, n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
If n is prime, then (n-1)! ≡ -1 (mod n).
If n is composite and n > 4, then (n-1)! ≡ 0 (mod n).
For prime p, (p-2)! ≡ 1 (mod p).
For composite n > 4, n divides (n-1)!.
For prime p > 2, (p-1)! is odd and ≡ -1 (mod p).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or ((math.factorial(n-1) % 2 == 1) and (math.factorial(n-1) % n == n - 1))

claim: For prime p, (p-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p).
For prime p ≥ 5, (p-1)! ≡ -1 (mod 2p).
For composite n > 4, (n-1)! ≡ 0 (mod n), but for prime p, (p-1)! ≢ 0 (mod p).
predicate: ((is_prime(n)) or (n <= 4) or ((math.factorial(n-1) % n) == 0)) and ((not is_prime(n)) or ((math.factorial(n-1) % n) != 0))

claim: For prime p, (p-2)! (p-1) ≡ -1 (mod p).
For composite n where n = pq with p, q primes and 2 < p ≤ q < n, we have p | (n-1)!.
For prime p, gcd(p, (p-1)!) = 1.
For composite n > 4, gcd(n, (n-1)!) = n.
n is prime if and only if gcd(n, (n-1)!) = 1.
For prime p, (p-1)! ≡ -1 (mod p^2) if and only if p is a Wilson prime.
For prime p > 2, (p-1)! is odd.
For prime p > 2, (p-1)! + 1 is even.
For prime p, ((p-1)!)^2 ≡ 1 (mod p).
For odd prime p, (p-1)! ≡ ±1 (mod p).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or ((math.factorial(n-1) % n == 1) or (math.factorial(n-1) % n == n - 1))

claim: For odd prime p, (p-1)! ≡ -1 (mod p) (strengthening of Wilson's theorem).

predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or (((math.factorial((n-1)//2) * math.factorial((n+1)//2)) % n) == (pow(-1, (n+1)//2, n)))

claim: For prime p ≥ 5, vp((p-1)!, p) = 0 (where vp is the p-adic valuation).
For composite n, vp((n-1)!, n) > 0 for some prime divisor p of n.
predicate: (is_prime(n)) or (any(vp(math.factorial(n-1), p) > 0 for p in [q for q in range(2, n) if is_prime(q) and n % q == 0]))

claim: For prime p, phi(p-1) divides the exponent of the multiplicative group mod p.
For prime p, tau(p-1) counts divisors of p-1, which partition the residues coprime to p.
For prime p > 2, (p-1) has at least two distinct prime divisors (except p = 3).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 2) or (n == 3) or (len([p for p in range(2, n-1) if is_prime(p) and (n-1) % p == 0]) >= 2)

claim: For prime p, (p-1)! and p are coprime.
If n is composite and n ≠ 4, then some prime q < n divides both n and (n-1)!.
predicate: (is_prime(n)) or (n == 4) or (any(q < n and is_prime(q) and n % q == 0 and math.factorial(n-1) % q == 0 for q in range(2, n)))

claim: For prime p and any integer a with gcd(a, p) = 1, a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (all(pow(a, n-1, n) == 1 for a in range(1, min(n, 100)) if gcd(a, n) == 1))

claim: For prime p > 2, 2^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p).
For prime p, the product of all quadratic residues mod p divides p! / ((p-1)/2)!.
For prime p ≡ 1 (mod 4), ((p-1)/2)! ≡ √(-1) (mod p) has a solution.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n % 4 != 1) or (any(pow(x, 2, n) == n - 1 for x in range(1, n)))

claim: For prime p > 2, (p-1)! + 1 is divisible by 2 but not 4.
For prime p > 2, (p-1)! + 1 ≡ 2 (mod 4).
For prime p and integer k ≥ 1, (pk - 1)! ≡ (-1)^{k-1} (p-1)!^k (mod p).
For prime p, sum_{i=1}^{p-1} i ≡ 0 (mod p).
For prime p, sum_{i=1}^{p-1} i^2 ≡ 0 (mod p) if p ≠ 2.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or (sum(pow(i, 2, n) for i in range(1, n)) % n == 0)

claim: For prime p, sum_{i=1}^{p-1} (i-1)! ≡ -1 (mod p).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (sum(math.factorial(max(0, i-1)) for i in range(1, n)) % n == n - 1)
(not is_prime(n)) or (sum(pow(i, n-1, n) for i in range(1, n)) % n == n - 1)

claim: For prime p, the set {1, 2, ..., p-1} forms a group under multiplication mod p.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (all(gcd(i, n) == 1 for i in range(1, n)))

claim: For prime p, every non-identity element of (Z/pZ)* has order dividing p-1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (all(pow(a, n-1, n) == 1 for a in range(1, n) if gcd(a, n) == 1))

claim: For prime p > 2, (-1) is a quadratic residue mod p if and only if p ≡ 1 (mod 4).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or ((n % 4 == 1) == any(pow(x, 2, n) == n - 1 for x in range(1, n)))

claim: For prime p ≡ 3 (mod 4), -1 is not a quadratic residue mod p.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n % 4 != 3) or (not any(pow(x, 2, n) == n - 1 for x in range(1, n)))

claim: For prime p, sigma(p-1) = sum of divisors of p-1.
For prime p, sigma(p-1) > p-1 (p-1 is deficient).
For prime p, tau(p-1) divides the multiplicative group order.
For prime p ≥ 5, (p-2)! ≡ (p-1) (mod p^2) if and only if p is a Wilson prime.
For prime p, the exponent of the multiplicative group (Z/pZ)* equals p-1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (all(pow(a, n-1, n) == 1 for a in range(1, min(n, 50)) if gcd(a, n) == 1))

claim: For composite n > 4, every prime divisor p of n satisfies p < n and p divides (n-1)!.
predicate: (is_prime(n)) or (n <= 4) or (all(p >= 2 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0) and all(math.factorial(n-1) % p == 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0))

claim: n is prime if and only if the multiplicative group (Z/nZ)* is cyclic of order n-1.
predicate: (is_prime(n)) == (all(pow(a, n-1, n) == 1 for a in range(1, min(n, 50)) if gcd(a, n) == 1))
